finding.contract_claim: 3 implicit contracts between src/osoji/llm/claude_code.py and tests/test_claude_code_provider.py: "claude-code", "claudeMdExcludes", "end_turn"
finding.observed_behavior: Extract shared constants to a common module so a rename triggers an import error instead of a silent mismatch
finding.triage_reasoning:
Examined all three string literals across both files, plus their usage in the broader codebase.

**"claude-code"**
- Defined in `claude_code.py` as a bare return value (`return "claude-code"`) and in `_parse_response` as a model fallback (`model=model_name or "claude-code"`).
- Appears in 10+ files throughout the project (`config.py`, `init.py`, `rate_limiter.py`, `factory.py`, `registry.py`, etc.).
- `OpenAIProvider` and `OpenRouterProvider` use a `_PROVIDER_NAME = "openai"` class-variable pattern; `ClaudeCodeProvider` does not — a real style inconsistency.
- Test asserts `p.name == "claude-code"` (line 83) and tests the prefix-stripping overload (line 361).
- Any drift between production and test causes an explicit `AssertionError`, not a silent mismatch. The "silent mismatch" framing does not hold for test assertions.
- The real codebase concern (10+ bare literals with no central constant) is broader than the specific test↔production claim.

**"claudeMdExcludes"**
- Appears only once in production code (`--settings` JSON payload at line 267) and once in the test as a substring check (line 627).
- This is a Claude Code CLI parameter name — an external protocol string the project does not control. Falls squarely in the ecosystem-convention category: the CLI binary defines the schema, not this project.
- Extracting it to a constant would name something the project has no authority to rename.

**"end_turn"**
- Set as the hardcoded stop-reason in `_parse_response` (`stop_reason="end_turn"`) and mapped from Google's "STOP" in `google.py:23`.
- Originates from the Anthropic API vocabulary — an ecosystem convention. `google.py` explicitly maps a foreign value (Google's "STOP") to this string, confirming it's treated as a cross-provider normalization target, not a locally-owned constant.
- No production code ever branches on "end_turn" specifically (only "length" is consumed). A mismatch between files would have no behavioral consequence.
- Test assertion `result.stop_reason == "end_turn"` is an explicit check; drift fails loudly.

**Common flaw in the claim:** The premise is "a rename triggers a silent mismatch." All three literals appear only in test assertions. Test assertions are definitionally not silent — they produce AssertionError at run time. Extracting to shared constants would convert a test-run failure (AssertionError) to an import-collection failure (ImportError), a marginal DX improvement, not a correctness fix. None of the three literals constitutes a latent bug or a risk of undetected divergence in deployed behaviour.
